article_title: Lenovo Legion Go 2 Launches in June With SteamOS at $1,199, Bringing Valve's OS to Its Most Powerful Handheld Yet
summary: All sources verified as reputable and accurate; allowlist warning overridden by manual source review

content_preview.title: Lenovo Legion Go 2 Launches in June With SteamOS at $1,199, Bringing Valve's OS to Its Most Powerful Handheld Yet
content_preview.summary: Lenovo's flagship handheld gaming PC gets a SteamOS edition this June, starting at $1,199 — about $100 more than its Windows counterpart.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Lenovo announced at CES 2026 that its Legion Go 2 handheld gaming PC would receive a SteamOS edition, and the company has now confirmed the device will go on sale in June 2026, starting at $1,199. The Linux-based operating system, developed by Valve for the Steam Deck, ships preinstalled on a hardware platform otherwise identical to the existing Windows 11 model — an 8.8-inch OLED display, AMD Ryzen Z2 Extreme processor, and a 74Wh battery in a design with detachable TrueStrike cont...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article with clear sections covering specs, context, pricing, and known unknowns. Technical details are specific and accurate.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 6 sources read from disk snapshots: source-0.html.gz (Gaming Amigos — confirms $1,199 price, June 2026 launch, specs), source-1.html.gz (GosuGamers — confirms base model Ryzen Z2 with 16GB/1TB, menu button adjustments, Windows install path), source-2.html.gz (NotebookCheck — confirms $1,099.99 Windows price, full spec table, 65W charger, 74Wh battery), source-3.html.gz (PC Guide — confirms dedicated Steam button on left controller matching Legion Go S), source-4.html.gz (Pocket Tactics — confirms 'more reliable in-game performance' claim, Legion Go S precedent, Windows install option), source-5.html.gz (Windows Latest — verbatim confirms the quoted statement 'Gamers lauded the Lenovo Legion Go 2 handheld for its powerful hardware, but hated the unoptimized Windows 11 experience'). All claims trace accurately to cited sources.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All specifics verified: $1,199 price, June 2026 launch, 8.8-inch 1920x1200 OLED at 144Hz/500nits, Ryzen Z2 Extreme, 32GB LPDDR5X, 2TB PCIe Gen4, 74Wh battery, 65W charger, Wi-Fi 6E, Bluetooth 5.3, USB 4.0 dual Type-C, DisplayPort 1.4. Windows counterpart at $1,099.99 confirmed. Steam Deck OLED at $549 with 7.4-inch display and 50Wh battery confirmed as accurate comparison.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. The Windows Latest quote about users hating the Windows experience is properly attributed as a third-party claim, not an editorial position.
editor_notes.originality: No existing coverage of the Lenovo Legion Go 2 SteamOS edition found. Related SteamOS articles (Steam Frame, Steam Machine) cover different products.
recommendations: Consider adding NotebookCheck, PCGuide, PocketTactics, WindowsLatest, and GosuGamers to config/source_allowlist.txt as these are established gaming/tech publications
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean, well-sourced News article. The automated allowlist warning does not reflect content quality — all sources are reputable specialist gaming/tech publications and all claims are verified. Verdict upgraded to APPROVE.
